rows.scopeSummary:
The objective of the CCS (Convergent Charging System) Phase 2 project is that the UK Government regulation mandates the decommission of the Huawei Online Charging System (OCS) platform from the Virgin Media O2 (VMO2) network. To achieve Phase 2 of CCS, the project will: 
• Augment the initial CCS charging platform implementation to provide one combined charging system for VMO2 supporting all mobile subscribers. 
• Migrate all PAYM subscribers currently on OCS onto the CCS platform by November 2026 to enable OCS decommissioning from December 2026. 

For the purposes of baselining, the TCS estimates only cover the activities from the period of 06 January 2026 to 31 March 2026 (Phase 2 of CCS). The scope of this work covers : 
1. Scope Item 1 : Requirement grooming and Data mapping document for CCS PH2 release 
2.  Scope Item 2: GCP Native Inbound Data Integration Delivery for CCS PH2 release 
3. Scope Item 3 : GCP Native Outbound Data Integration - ICD Documentation and Delivery for CCS PH2 release 
4. Scope Item 4 : System Integration Test (SIT)/End to End (E2E) Test Support for CCS PH2 release 
5. Scope Item 5 : Deployment and Early Life Data Support for CCS PH2 March release

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 7: CCS Phase 2

rows.scopeSummary:
The Qualys tool, specifically Strategic Qualys, is designed to automatically discover and categorize both known and unknown assets, including those within the internal network and those exposed to the internet. It continuously identifies unmanaged assets, offering real-time alerts on vulnerabilities, compromised devices, and network irregularities. Strategic Qualys has been chosen as the preferred tool for VMO2, replacing the previous "O2 Qualys" vulnerability solution, to provide ongoing, automated vulnerability detection across VMO2's hybrid infrastructure. This will encompass both VM and O2 assets, all managed within the same platform.

The goal of this integration is to connect the Strategic Configuration Management Database (CMDB) with Qualys, enabling a two-way data exchange for Configuration Items (CIs). This ensures that the data within Qualys remains accurate and aligned with the business inventory. By enhancing host data in Qualys, this integration will improve vulnerability management and enable a more effective response to security events that impact specific applications and hardware.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 9: O2 Helix CMDB Integration

rows.scopeSummary:
Automated Commissions Engine (ACE) is a comprehensive system designed to handle the complete lifecycle of commission processing for the external partners; from transactional and reference data ingestion, data processing, commissions configuration and calculations, report generation and publishing for partners to access and partner query management. ACE is currently hosted in SAP cloud. It includes two servers:
1. ACE Landing Pad - VMO2 data preprocessing application layer, built in a unix
server in SAP cloud on Oracle and Informatica Powercenter
2. SAP Commission engine, which is an SAP product for commissions configuration 
and calculation. 
The purpose of this IA is to perform the GCP environment (platform, infrastructure, connectivity) set up, low-level design of the platform migration and low-level design of the data migration for this work.

rows.scopeSummary:
The GPLB Switching process will allow business customers to follow an industry-agreed GPLS process for the following Services:
• Fixed Line Number
• Broadband
Customers will provide the current Service Provider details at the point of sale and provide consent for services to be switched. The LP will confirm the impacts of switching directly to the Customer. The GP will process the switch request and any related number porting on behalf of Customers. This will enable customers to have quick, easy, and reliable switching.
This engagement is to provide data solution for the matching of customer data, services and provide necessary responses. This engagement is specific for SoHo and SoHo_A&P customer.

rows.scopeSummary:
Project Picasso is intended to deliver a refreshed set of fixed front book bundles in September 2025, the new front book should deliver improvements in choice and flexibility for customers whilst addressing some known challenges that we have today. The front book should be streamlining the range where possible, removing products that have low appeal and sales, addressing any illogical up-sell or cross-sell price steps and resolving the high ongoing bundle pricing that causes bill shock when offers end. There is a desire to include third-party SVOD content (primarily Netflix) with bundles if supported by a suitable commercial agreement, there is also a desire to sell value accretive add-ons (Bundles of content add on) to grow ARPU in the purchase journey. This will all be supported by a reimagining of the current Digital sales experience, to give the impression of modularity, flexibility and greater customer choice.
Based on the Picasso requirement for Anaplan reporting, we need to include a new field in the fact tables to derive the SKY UHD movements in the fact Coc tables, along with the corresponding start and end values in the fact_wfr tables.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 17: Picasso

rows.scopeSummary:
VMO2’s Enterprise Data Hub (EDH) is a centralised, unified data platform which provides business critical data across VMO2’s organisation. Originally EDH was built as an on-premise solution hosted on Cloudera Data Platform but has recently been migrated onto Cloudera Data Platform (CDP) hosted on Google Cloud Platform (GCP) in a ‘lift and shift’ migration onto virtual machines. This migration has allowed VMO2 to move to the Cloud quickly and decouple from licensing and hardware constraints present with the previous on-premise solution. However, this has come at the cost of an architecture which involves higher operational costs than if EDH was built on GCP native technology.
Therefore, VMO2 has decided to migrate EDH on CDP GCP to GCP cloud native to fully realise the cost benefits of a cloud native solution and to align the EDH’s end state infrastructure with VMO2’s strategic technology vision for 2024 and beyond.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 20: EDH > GCP

rows.scopeSummary:
The Netpulse is a Cloudera Hadoop based Bigdata and analytics platform which enables the business to collect, store and analyze a vast amount of complex data from varied sources of mobile network elements that are internal and external to VMO2. The platform helps the business gain critical insights into the overall customer experience, usage patterns and quality of services provided by the business to its customer base.

Netpulse is a large on-premises Hadoop based platform running business critical data warehouse and analytics capabilities in the former O2 network domain. The insights provided by the platform are increasingly playing a critical role in strategic decision-making across the organization; therefore, any occurrence of service disruption have the potential to impact the quality and delivery timelines of critical business operations and projects.

As it stands, the platform stores up to 6 Petabytes of data in Hadoop cluster, available for analytics using Hive, Spark, Python, and native Hadoop libraries. The platform ingests more than 10 Terabytes of source data feeds on daily basis and processes billions of transactions from Network, CRM, and other external sources per day. 
 
The Netpulse Hadoop cluster provides cumulative compute capacity of 6480 CPU Cores and 45 TB Memory space in production environment and 684 CPU Cores and 4.9 TB Memory space in Testbed environment.

Netpulse ingestion layer is built using three different solutions, 
1. Cardinality solution distributed across 80+ physical servers in production and 20+ physical servers in testbed. 
2. HDF platform (NiFi) with 896 CPU cores in production and 264 CPU cores in Testbed.
3. Unix script-based ingestion.

Due to increasingly wider adaption of the platform for varying use cases across the business, its footprint and complexity has significantly grown over time, making the task of maintaining the resilience and operational standards incredibly challenging without causing disruption or degradation to its core services.

While Netpulse platform provides the capability to monitor many metrics, it is not a simple monitoring tool. By integrating many disparate data sources & applying complex analytics, it delivers predictive capabilities and tools replacing outdated methods of collecting and analyzing data (e.g., the VDT can replace traditional drive testing). Netpulse is the only tool across VMO2 that pulls data from enough sources to create a true picture of performance at both a network and, importantly, at a customer (MSISDN) level. Through Netpulse analytics, the radio team can identify cells of concern and focus efforts in those areas to provide quick wins and noticeable improvements in network quality for VMO2 customers. In short, Netpulse generates predictive models (e.g., NCX predictor) to drive customer satisfaction through targeted network improvements and enhanced customer service.

Therefore, Netpulse is a key platform for VMO2 data analytics strategy to refine and enhance VMO2 products including predictive analytics. Netpulse will play a key role in improving VMO2 customers satisfaction journey. Hence, we intend to migrate Netpulse into GCP to increase flexibility & scalability, achieve simplified management & monitoring, adapt innovative technologies, elastic approach and optimize costs.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 21: Netpulse > GCP

rows.scopeSummary:
Release of MyO2 app built on App Factory. App Factory is a single app code base. The aim of App Factory was to resolve this with core code shared across the apps, reducing the need to duplicate integration with Identity, Communications, Orchestration, Analytics, Chat, and other key SDKs.
MyO2 app is currently built on Novum. We want to rebuild the app as React Native (App Factory) and bring the development in-house.

To rebuild the MyO2 app using App Factory, leveraging an advanced tech stack aligned with VMO2's end-state architecture. This approach enables seamless integration of new features and enhancements powered by Engine, ensuring a future-ready, robust, and scalable app experience for our customers.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 22: My O2 Mobile App Rebuild

rows.scopeSummary:
This project focuses on the development of a new Pay As You Go (PayG) Transformation Web Application, built using NextJS. The application will encompass a comprehensive suite of customer journeys, including Home Page, Top-up, Payment Method management, SIM ordering and replacement, Bolt Ons, managing personal details, registration and sign-in, joining and leaving O2, tariff management, unblocking/unlocking services, document access, and contact preferences.

The project involves the creation of 35-40 dynamic web pages, implementation of 100 analytics tags, and integration with numerous third-party systems such as Experian & GCP, Autopopup Portal, Rolling Plan Portal, O2 Rewards, Blackhawk, CCS, MiPay, CPS, Real Digital, and O2 Identity. Technical components include Google Analytics and Braze SDKs, 47 API developments and orchestrations, Dynatrace for monitoring and alerting, and CDO APIgee and Microservices for DFE PAYG. The scope covers end-to-end delivery from requirement gathering through to warranty support, including comprehensive functional, performance, security, and API testing, alongside test data creation.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 24: PayG Transformation

rows.scopeSummary: This project is dedicated to transforming Pay As You Go (PayG) customer communications. It involves the creation and management of 108 personalised communication templates across 43 canvases, delivered via Email, SMS, and Print channels. A key focus is on dynamic personalisation using existing data attributes and the creation of two new real-time audiences. The scope includes implementing 108 event-driven or scheduled communications, a bidirectional communication flow for Balance Enquiry, and comprehensive end-to-end delivery, testing, and post-go-live monitoring.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 25: PayG Communication

rows.scopeSummary:

The project covers end-to-end delivery from requirement gathering to warranty support, including comprehensive functional, performance, security, and API testing. Post-go-live, there will be delivery and engagement monitoring, with feedback sent back to Netcracker and SOA for necessary changes. Basic analytics for delivery, open, and click-through rates will also be implemented.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 26: BSS Service Comms

rows.scopeSummary:
The O2 Transformation project involves a migration from the legacy Netcracker 360 platform to a new technology stack utilising NextJS for static content and Angular. The project implements ElasticSearch for the upper funnel catalogue, which is populated by stock and pricing data from Netcracker (BSS) via daily CSV files. An MSA service is responsible for merging and processing this data, which is then consumed by the front end to display consolidated stock and pricing information.

Netcracker’s catalogue is out of scope. The digital sales catalogue will ensure products from Netcracker are integrated, with the upper funnel, basket logic, and checkout experience built as part of the transformation Order processing continues to be managed by Netcracker, including subsequent communications, handling, and fulfilment via the PAU process. The current MSA does not queue orders after they are sent from the order entry.​ There are no queues, such as dead letter or message queues, for orders.​
Performance and accessibility testing is in scope and must meet AAA standards. Internal APIs retrieve data from systems like Netcracker, while external APIs are exposed to the front end to drive functionality.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 27: O2.co.uk Transformation

rows.scopeSummary: This project aims to transform O2's marketing capabilities by implementing and leveraging a new platform, primarily Braze, for 11-50 users (Campaign Managers, Merkle, Platform Engineering). The scope includes designing and building 17 event-based and data-triggered customer journeys across four channels (SMS, Email, MMS, Web Content Card). It involves significant data work, including 51-100 Data Landing Tables, creation of 20+ new audiences and integrating one Recommendation Engine with Braze. The project also covers front-end and API client-side SDK integration, handling three internal inbound events, creating 21-100 communication templates, and comprehensive testing for language, locale, analytics, and tagging.

provenance.evidenceSpan: TCS sheet row 28: O2 Marketing Transformation
